description:
Upstream bmad-master.agent.yaml has menu (LT/LW triggers) and critical_actions (greeting with /bmad-help). These don't map to Claude Code agent format. Since we enable all features, embed everything as instructions in the agent markdown:

- Menu items → tell agent about available /bmad: skills
- critical_actions → greeting instructions mentioning /bmad:help
- hasSidecar: false → not applicable

Low priority: the master agent works without these. Revisit when modularizing the plugin.

Files: plugins/bmad/agents/bmad-master.md, scripts/generate-agents.ts
title: Embed bmad-master menu and critical_actions as agent instructions

description:
Multiple README issues found on main branch after v6.0.0-Beta.8.0 release. All fixes go in scripts/update-readme-version.ts which already uses comment markers (upstream-version-start/end) to replace content.

Issues:
1. Badge still shows Beta.7 — badge is likely outside the markers. Move badge inside markers or add separate badge markers so the script updates it too.
2. Sync upstream source table is broken — investigate what renders wrong on main.
3. Repo column should use hyperlinks — line 38 writes source.repo as plain text. Wrap in markdown link: [repo](https://github.com/repo).
4. Date column should show upstream module release date, not sync date — line 28 uses new Date() (today). Read the tag date from git instead.

Since we already use comment markers for the table, extend the same pattern for badges — wrap badges in their own marker pair so the script can update them too.
title: Fix README: badge, upstream source table, repo hyperlinks, date column

close_reason: Resolved differently: single script with --after-ci flag instead of splitting into two scripts
description:
The current release.sh has a natural pause point: after creating the PR (human reviews, CI runs) and before merging. Split into two scripts:

1. release-prepare.sh: Validate on dev branch, optionally bump version, sync beads, create release branch, push, create PR targeting main. Outputs PR URL/number.

2. release-finalize.sh <pr-number>: Wait for CI, merge PR, tag main, create GitHub release, return to dev.

Background: The original release.sh tried to push directly to main which is protected. It was rewritten in v6.0.0-Beta.7.0 as a single script with gh pr checks --watch, but two scripts is cleaner — there's a natural human checkpoint between prepare and finalize.
title: Split release.sh into prepare and finalize scripts

description: claude plugin update doesn't git pull the marketplace cache before comparing versions, so it reports stale versions as latest. Upstream: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/28540 (also #14061, #25244, #25598 as duplicates). Workaround in README Installation section. When fixed upstream, remove the Troubleshooting section from README. Add a weekly CI check (Thursdays) using gh API to see if #28540 is closed: gh issue view 28540 --repo anthropics/claude-code --json state -q .state
title: Track Claude Code plugin update bug fix (anthropics/claude-code#28540)

close_reason: Closed
description:
Create .claude/bmad.local.md with YAML frontmatter for per-project BMAD configuration. Settings from upstream core/module.yaml and bmm/module.yaml:

- user_name: what agents call the user
- communication_language: chat language
- document_output_language: doc output language
- output_folder: base output directory (default: _bmad-output)
- project_name: project name for artifacts
- user_skill_level: beginner/intermediate/expert
- planning_artifacts: where PRDs, briefs, architecture docs go
- implementation_artifacts: where sprint docs go
- project_knowledge: where long-term docs go (default: docs/)

File format: .claude/bmad.local.md with YAML frontmatter (auto-gitignored by Claude Code). Agents read this at activation to know where to write artifacts and how to communicate.

This is essential for agent-as-service architecture — the settings file is the shared coordination mechanism between independent BMAD agents.
title: Implement BMAD project settings file (.claude/bmad.local.md)

close_reason: Fixed: removed badge JSON push steps from sync-upstream.yml, issue creation no longer blocked by protected branch
description:
The sync-upstream.yml workflow runs on main and attempts to git push badge JSON updates directly to main. This fails because main is a protected branch (requires PRs).

Error: 'GH006: Protected branch update failed for refs/heads/main — Changes must be made through a pull request.'

The latest failure shows upstream core is at v6.0.0-Beta.8, but the badge commit was rejected. This also explains why the 'Synced with BMAD-METHOD' workflow badge shows a red failure state on the README.

Two sub-problems:
1. Workflow cannot push to main — needs to either create a PR, or run on dev instead
2. Badge URLs in README point to main branch, so they show stale versions until a release merge

Options to fix:
A. Change workflow to run on dev, change badge URLs to read from dev
B. Change workflow to create a PR instead of direct push
C. Add a GitHub repo rule exception for github-actions[bot] on main
title: Sync workflow fails: cannot push badge updates to protected main branch

Test by installing plugin, bumping version, and checking marketplace behavior.

description: The agent.customize.template.yaml defines principles as an array (principles: []). The new qa.agent.yaml correctly uses a YAML array. But all older agents (analyst, architect, dev, pm, sm, quick-flow-solo-dev, ux-designer, bmad-master, tech-writer) use principles: | (block scalar string). This inconsistency broke our generate-agents.ts script when qa was added in v6.0.0-Beta.7. Options: (a) PR upstream to normalize all agents to arrays (correct per template), (b) PR upstream to change qa to string (match majority). Recommend (a). Ref: commit 7ecae1d0 introduced qa.agent.yaml.
title: Upstream: agent YAML principles field inconsistency (string vs array)

description:
Research how skill files should reference module config, then implement the rewrite.

Upstream skills reference {project-root}/_bmad/<module>/config.yaml to load module configuration. Our plugin replaced config collection with .claude/bmad.local.md (bmp-2ry, closed), but 69 path references in skill step files still point to old config.yaml paths.

Research (Claude Code topic — delegate to Claude Code Handbook session):
1. When an agent invokes a skill, does the agent's context carry over? (If yes, config is already loaded)
2. Can skill .md files reference .claude/bmad.local.md directly?
3. Does ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} resolve inside skill step content?

See .git-ignored/claude-code-research.md R4 for full research spec.

Deliverables:

2. Implement the config.yaml path rewrite (remove, replace, or redirect) in generate-skills.ts
3. Regenerate and verify

description:
Design the storage layout for BMAD agent sidecar data.

Key questions:
1. Where should bmad_data live? Options: ~/.claude/projects/<path>/bmad_data/, .claude/bmad_data/ in project, or plugin-relative
2. What folder structure? Per-agent (bmad_data/tech-writer/) or per-topic?
3. How do agents discover their data folder path?
4. Read/write permissions — can plugin agents write to this location?

Expected output: A proposed directory layout with rationale. Must account for: multiple agents, cross-session persistence, not auto-loaded, writable by agents.

Depends on findings from the two research issues above (auto-memory behavior + agent file references).

close_reason: Closed
description:
Decision: Remove disable-model-invocation: true from ALL 62 skills.

Rationale: BMAD workflows have built-in step-by-step guidance. Blocking model invocation prevents agents (like bmad-master) from orchestrating workflows. Users still control the flow even when agents invoke skills.

Implementation:
1. Edit scripts/generate-skills.ts — remove the line that adds disable-model-invocation: true to SKILL.md frontmatter
2. Regenerate all skills: bun run generate:skills --source core && bun run generate:skills --source tea && bun run generate:skills --source bmb && bun run generate:skills --source cis && bun run generate:skills --source gds
3. Verify: grep -r "disable-model-invocation" plugins/bmad/skills/ should return 0 results

Files:
- scripts/generate-skills.ts (edit template)
- plugins/bmad/skills/*/SKILL.md (62 files, regenerated by script)
title: Fix: all skills have disable-model-invocation: true — decide which should be model-invocable

description:
Resolve skill name collisions across modules and implement the path rewrite for workflow references.

Problem: When multiple upstream modules have workflows with the same name (e.g., bmm/code-review and gds/code-review), our plugin uses a gds- prefix workaround. Path rewrites need to handle:

- {project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/code-review/ → ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/code-review/
- {project-root}/_bmad/gds/workflows/code-review/ → ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/gds-code-review/

14 skills use this workaround (see pre-push validation warnings).

Deliverables:
1. Decide: is gds- prefix permanent, or adopt a different convention?
2. Build a mapping table from upstream module+workflow path → plugin skill name
3. Implement path rewrite function in generate-skills.ts for workflow references
4. Regenerate and verify

This is the largest category (~380 workflow path references).

Child of bmp-n3f.
title: Resolve skill name collisions across modules (GDS prefix workaround)

description:
Determine exactly what Claude Code auto-loads from the projects directory (~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/).

Key questions:
1. What files/folders are auto-loaded into context? Only memory/MEMORY.md (first 200 lines), or more?
2. Can we create sibling folders next to memory/ (e.g., bmad_data/)? Will Claude Code ignore them?
3. What is the official name for the ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/ directory? (session context path?)
4. Does anything else in that directory get auto-loaded (settings, state, etc.)?

Expected output: A factual summary with citations to Claude Code docs or source code. Include any relevant configuration options.

Context: We need a place to store BMAD agent sidecar data (per-agent knowledge files) that persists across sessions but is NOT auto-loaded into context. The data should be fetchable on demand via file reads in agent instructions.
title: Research: Claude Code auto-memory loading behavior

close_reason: Added getLastSyncDate() using git log, added Last Synced column to README table
description:
scripts/update-readme-version.ts always sets Last synced to today's date, even when the upstream version hasn't changed. After bmp-t5o creates the multi-source table, each row will have its own Last Synced column.

Fix: Only update a source's "Last Synced" date when its .upstream-version-* file content actually changed.

Approach (recommended): Use git log to find when each version file was last modified:
  git log -1 --format=%ai -- .upstream-version-core | cut -d' ' -f1

This gives the date of the last commit that touched the version file. No extra tracking files needed.

Alternative: Compare version before/after in the script, but this only works during a single run and doesn't handle historical dates.

Files to modify:
- scripts/update-readme-version.ts — use git log date for each source's Last Synced column

Depends on: bmp-t5o (table format must exist first)
title: Fix Last synced date: only update when upstream version actually changes

description: With multiple upstreams (core, TEA, future CIS/GDS/BMB), the current core-anchored versioning needs a clear decision. Questions: How do we communicate which upstream versions are included? Should .upstream-version-* files drive version metadata? How does plugin version relate to individual module versions? Document the decision.
title: Decide multi-upstream versioning strategy

description:
Replace upstream's {project-root}/_bmad/_memory/ sidecar system with a Claude Code-native approach for persistent per-agent knowledge files.

Upstream's sidecar pattern: each agent has a folder at _bmad/_memory/<agent>-sidecar/ containing instructions.md, memories.md, documentation-standards.md, etc. Agents read/write these across sessions.

56 path references in generated skills point to _memory/ paths. These cannot be simple path rewrites — they need a design decision on where agent knowledge lives in our plugin.

Depends on research: bmp-dvz (auto-memory behavior), bmp-tv2 (agent file references), bmp-a1d (bmad_data design).

Blocks: bmp-n3f (full path rewrite completion).
title: Implement sidecar memory replacement for plugin agents

description:
Review the GDS workflowWorkarounds in scripts/lib/upstream-sources.ts (lines 134-151). There are 14 entries mapping GDS workflow names to gds-prefixed names to avoid collisions with core/TEA.

Task:
1. For each workaround entry, compare the GDS workflow content against the core/TEA original
2. Categorize each as: (a) true duplicate, (b) GDS-specific extension, (c) minor variation
3. For true duplicates: consider whether the workaround is still needed or if we should use the core version
4. Document findings in the issue notes field

Workarounds to review:
- document-project, generate-project-context (collisions with core phase-3)
- code-review, correct-course, create-story, dev-story, quick-dev, quick-spec, retrospective, sprint-planning, sprint-status (collisions with core phase-4/quick-flow)
- automate, test-design, test-review (collisions with TEA)

Files to read:
- .upstream/BMAD-METHOD/src/bmm/workflows/ (core originals)
- .upstream/bmad-module-game-dev-studio/src/workflows/ (GDS versions)
- .upstream/bmad-method-test-architecture-enterprise/src/workflows/testarch/ (TEA versions)

This is a research task — no code changes expected. Document findings and recommend actions.
title: Review GDS workflowWorkarounds for mismatches

close_reason: Closed
description:
The sync workflow (.github/workflows/sync-upstream.yml) has 5 jobs: check-core, check-tea, check-bmb, check-cis, check-gds. Only check-core has an "Update badge JSON" step (lines 41-56). The other 4 jobs are missing badge updates.

Fix: Add a badge update step to each of check-tea, check-bmb, check-cis, check-gds, following the same pattern as check-core.

Pattern (from check-core lines 41-56):
  - name: Update badge JSON
    if: steps.compare.outputs.changed == 'true'
    run: |
      cat > .github/badges/upstream-version-{id}.json <<EOF
      { "schemaVersion": 1, "label": "{Label} Module", "message": "$UPSTREAM", "color": "blue" }
      EOF
      git config user.name "github-actions[bot]" ...
      git add ... && git commit ... && git push

Badge files and labels:
- check-tea → .github/badges/upstream-version-tea.json, label "TEA Module"
- check-bmb → .github/badges/upstream-version-bmb.json, label "BMB Module"
- check-cis → .github/badges/upstream-version-cis.json, label "CIS Module"
- check-gds → .github/badges/upstream-version-gds.json, label "GDS Module"

Also: core badge (.github/badges/upstream-version.json) currently shows "v6.0.0-Beta.4" (stale) — update it to v6.0.0-Beta.7 as part of this change.

Files to modify:
1. .github/workflows/sync-upstream.yml — add badge update step to 4 jobs
2. .github/badges/upstream-version.json — fix stale version (v6.0.0-Beta.4 → v6.0.0-Beta.7)
title: Update sync workflow to auto-update module badges

description:
Determine how Claude Code plugin agents can reference and load external data files on demand.

Key questions:
1. Can agent .md files use @import or similar directives to pull in files at runtime?
2. Does ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} work inside agent instructions for file paths?
3. What is the mechanism for an agent to "fetch" a file only when needed (not pre-loaded)?
4. Can agents reference files outside the plugin directory (e.g., in ~/.claude/projects/)?

Expected output: Working examples of agents loading external files. Document what works and what does not.

Context: BMAD upstream agents have "sidecar memory" — per-agent knowledge files they read/write. We need our plugin agents to load these on demand without polluting the auto-loaded context.
title: Research: how plugin agents reference external data files

description: Currently 5 separate .upstream-version-* files at repo root. Merge into a single file (e.g., .upstream-versions.json). Update all scripts that read/write these files: sync-upstream-content.ts, bump-core.ts, bump-module.ts, bump-utils.ts, release.sh, sync-upstream.yml workflow, validate script, and update-readme script.
title: Consolidate upstream version files into single file

description: Upstream BMAD bundles content authoring (agents, workflows, skills) with IDE-specific installation (command generators, manifest CSVs). Our plugin proves these can be separated: upstream writes the methodology content, plugins handle platform-specific delivery. Research whether proposing this separation upstream would benefit the ecosystem — could our plugin architecture serve as a reference implementation?
title: Research: propose upstream BMAD separation of content authoring vs IDE installation
